title: givotia stipularis
intro: Givotia stipularis, also known as the Indian olive or Ceylon ironwood, is a large tree native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Givotia stipularis include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Givotia stipularis can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
